replies.author: @BigBossBernie
replies.text:
Seeing console prizes for PS5 increasing, games now selling for 80 bucks and poorly running full-price PC ports, Sony made "not buying" already the default choice.
They should once again learn to actually convince customers to invest time and money instead of preemptively calling non-buyer all the *isms they can think of.

Imagine Ford or GM bringing a new pick-up truck with twice the fuel consumption for double the market price with no improvements over the current generation and preemptively calling potential customers "tree huggers" or "poor" cause they now shit doesn't sell.

I appreciate the consideration.

author: @bryandedon7459
text: It's easy, or was easy, to push that shit with an established IP. People used to buy it bc they liked the one before. They would defend the changes bc the game before was good. It's a lot harder to get people to buy a new IP, especially when you destroyed the previous one that they liked. Just bc you're a known studio and have put out good games in the past doesn't matter anymore. It's gotten so much worse that even sequels from known studios are bombing now. They were lucky with the last of us 2. It came out at the beginning of all this dei bs. It's been years and people have only gotten more and more fed up with it.
